regulator.patient_advocate:
```json
{
  "strengths": [
    "Comprehensive revision addressing most prior critiques with specific evidence citations",
    "Realistic implementation timeline of 60 months with proper phases for development, capital raising, and CMS approval",
    "Proper statutory citation for Section 1115 waiver authority (42 USC 1315(a))",
    "Addition of mandatory patient protection compliance (42 CFR 438 Subpart F) addressing fatal regulatory flaw",
    "Actuarially sound capitation rate of $520 PMPM with detailed cost breakdown",
    "Addition of required capital reserve requirement (300% RBC ratio) for regulatory compliance",
    "Specific MLR target of 93% with appropriate risk corridor design",
    "Enhanced human oversight ratios (10:1 supervision, 25% synchronous review) appropriate for vulnerable population",
    "Explicit 340B preservation addressing FQHC participation requirements",
    "Mandatory comparative outcomes reporting to prevent two-tier medicine",
    "Conservative AI encounter share (40%) acknowledging digital divide constraints"
  ],
  "weaknesses": [
    {
      "domain": "regulatory_pathway",
      "issue": "AI licensure model lacks specific regulatory framework details",
      "suggested_value": "physician_supervised_with_specific_medical_board_protocols",
      "justification": "Medical board approval requires specific protocols for AI supervision, not just general approval. Need detailed scope of practice definitions per state medical practice acts."
    },
    {
      "domain": "clinical_delivery_model", 
      "issue": "85% confidence threshold for escalation lacks clinical validation",
      "suggested_value": "validated_clinical_decision_rules_plus_confidence_threshold",
      "justification": "Confidence thresholds alone insufficient - need validated clinical decision rules (e.g., Ottawa ankle rules, PECARN) integrated with AI confidence for legally defensible escalation criteria."
    },
    {
      "domain": "payment_structure",
      "issue": "Budget neutrality calculation methodology not specified as required",
      "suggested_value": "per_member_per_month_comparison_with_historical_baseline",
      "justification": "42 CFR 431.412 requires specific budget neutrality methodology. Must specify historical baseline period and PMPM comparison methodology for CMS approval."
    },
    {
      "domain": "quality_measurement_framework",
      "issue": "No specification of network adequacy standards for virtual-first model",
      "suggested_value": "modified_time_distance_standards_for_virtual_plus_physical_access",
      "justification": "42 CFR 438.68 network adequacy cannot be waived. Need modified standards: virtual access within 24 hours, physical exam within 48 hours, specialist within 30 days."
    }
  ],
  "fatal_flaws": [
    {
      "domain": "regulatory_pathway",
      "issue": "Incomplete waiver scope specification",
      "why_fatal": "Section 1115 waiver application must specify exact regulatory provisions being waived. Current proposal doesn't identify which specific Medicaid managed care regulations (42 CFR 438) would be waived for AI clinical practice. Without this, CMS cannot evaluate waiver request."
    }
  ],
  "overall_assessment": "needs_revision"
}
```

actuary.patient_advocate:
```json
{
  "strengths": [
    "Comprehensive actuarial framework with 93% MLR target and mathematical justification",
    "Appropriate risk corridor design (±5% with 50% state sharing) per 42 CFR 438.6",
    "Realistic 300% RBC capital requirement ($62M for 100k members) meeting state insurance standards",
    "Evidence-based $520 PMPM capitation with detailed cost breakdown",
    "Conservative 10:1 supervision ratio with telemedicine supervision literature support",
    "Explicit 340B preservation addressing FQHC participation requirements",
    "60-month implementation timeline allowing adequate capital raising and development",
    "Structured escalation criteria (85% confidence threshold) providing operational clarity"
  ],
  "weaknesses": [
    {
      "domain": "payment_structure",
      "parameter": "startup_funding_source",
      "issue": "No specification of $62M capital source for RBC requirement",
      "suggested_value": "phased_capital_raise_24_months",
      "justification": "SOA studies show MCO startup requires 18-24 month capital raising period. Must specify philanthropic, state loan, or investor sources for $62M requirement."
    },
    {
      "domain": "clinical_delivery_model", 
      "parameter": "ibnr_reserve_methodology",
      "issue": "Missing IBNR calculation for AI encounter timing differences",
      "suggested_value": "15_pct_monthly_premium",
      "justification": "AI encounters may have different claim lag patterns than traditional care. Standard IBNR of 12-18% monthly premium needed for actuarial soundness."
    },
    {
      "domain": "payment_structure",
      "parameter": "medical_trend_assumption",
      "issue": "No medical trend factor in 5-year projection",
      "suggested_value": "4_5_pct_annual_trend",
      "justification": "CMS actuarial standards require medical trend assumptions. Medicaid trend averaging 4-5% annually (MACPAC 2024). Critical for rate adequacy."
    },
    {
      "domain": "organizational_structure",
      "parameter": "stop_loss_design",
      "issue": "Missing individual and aggregate stop-loss parameters",
      "suggested_value": "individual_250k_aggregate_110_pct_expected",
      "justification": "Essential for startup solvency. Individual stop-loss at $250k with 110% aggregate attachment standard for new plans per SOA guidance."
    }
  ],
  "fatal_flaws": [
    {
      "domain": "payment_structure",
      "parameter": "cash_flow_projection",
      "issue": "No monthly cash flow analysis for first 24 months",
      "why_fatal": "New ACO faces 60-90 day claim payment lag while paying providers monthly. Without cash flow projection, organization could become insolvent within 6 months despite adequate annual funding. This is the #1 cause of startup plan failure."
    },
    {
      "domain": "quality_measurement_framework",
      "parameter": "risk_adjustment_methodology", 
      "issue": "No specification of HCC, CDPS, or other risk adjustment system",
      "why_fatal": "Without risk adjustment, AI ACO will face severe adverse selection as sickest patients seek human care. CMS requires risk adjustment for all Medicaid managed care (42 CFR 438.6). Fatal for financial viability."
    }
  ],
  "overall_assessment": "needs_revision"
}
```
